questions.question: What is generative AI designed to do?
options: Only classify inputs into fixed categories
Create new content such as text or images
Only store and retrieve training examples

questions.question: How does a text-generating model typically produce an answer?
options: It searches the internet for the best paragraph
It copies and pastes a matching sentence from training data
It predicts the next tokens repeatedly until the output is complete
It applies fixed rules written by humans

questions.question: Which statement best matches how generative AI uses training data in normal use?
options: It always copies the training data word-for-word
It learns statistical relationships and combines concepts in new ways
It ignores the training data after training is finished
It only works if the prompt matches a training example exactly

questions.question: Which is a key risk of generative AI mentioned in the lesson?
options: It can produce plausible-sounding but incorrect information
It always produces the same output
It requires labeled data to run
